ground_truth.core_preferences: Conversational and confident. Benefits over features. Strong hooks that stop the scroll. Voice should feel like a smart friend, not a salesperson.
ground_truth.hidden_preferences: Headlines should create a TENSION that the body copy resolves — a headline that summarizes the product is a missed opportunity; the best headlines make you need to read the next line. Social proof should be woven into the narrative, not bolted on as a separate section — a customer quote mid-paragraph that reinforces the point you're making is stronger than a testimonials block at the bottom. Feature descriptions should always answer 'so what?' — the feature is what it does, the benefit is why you care, and the copy should spend most of its time on the benefit; listing features without translating them reads like a spec sheet. The call-to-action should feel like the natural next step, not a hard sell — it should flow from the copy above it so smoothly that NOT clicking feels like leaving something unfinished. Every landing page needs exactly one primary action — secondary CTAs compete with the main one and reduce conversion; if you can't decide what the one thing is, the strategy isn't clear yet.
ground_truth.dealbreakers: Hype words without substance behind them, making claims the product can't back up, talking down to the customer, copy that sounds like every other brand in the category
